Journal.Title: Huan jing ke xue= Huanjing kexue

Article.ArticleTitle: [Identification of Ecological Functions Zones Based on Ecosystem Service Bundles: A Case Study of the Ecological Demonstration Zone in Northwest Sichuan].

Abstract.AbstractText: Exploring the spatiotemporal evolution of ecosystem service synergies/trade-offs and determining ecologically functional zones based on ecosystem service bundles is crucial for guiding ecosystem management and territorial land optimization. Taking the Northwest Sichuan Ecological Demonstration Area as the study area, we first assessed four types of ecosystem services and then identified synergies/trade-offs among them through comprehensive correlation analysis and geographically weighted regression. Subsequently, we identified ecosystem service bundles and delineated ecologically functional zones using the self-organizing mapping network. Finally, the driving mechanism of ecosystem services in each ecologically functional zone was investigated through redundancy analysis. The major conclusions are: ① From 2000 to 2020, overall water and soil conservation services increased while the overall carbon storage and biodiversity maintenance functions decreased. ② All ecosystem service pairs exhibited a global synergistic relationship, whilst synergistic and trade-off relationships coexisted locally. ③ Based on the spatiotemporal evolution of ecosystem service bundles, the study area was divided into two ecologically functional zones: stable and fluctuating. These zones were further subdivided into nine sub-zones: dominant service, ecologically fragile, ecologically balanced, ecologically risky, key protection, ecological regulation, core zone, ecological restoration, and ecological enhancement. ④ Ecosystem services in each sub-zone were driven by a combination of natural-social factors, with meteorological and vegetation cover factors contributing the most to ecosystem services, followed by topographical factors and socio-economic factors. There were some differences in the drivers of ecosystem services in different ecologically functional sub-zones. This study provides a reference for the ecosystem service enhancement and sustainable development of the Northwest Sichuan Ecological Functional Area.
